title: Innodata Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Results
text: Innodata Inc. (Nasdaq: INOD) announced record Q1 2026 financial results, with revenue reaching $90.1 million, representing 54% year-over-year growth. The company reported adjusted EBITDA of $25.0 million (28% of revenue) and net income of $14.9 million, or $0.46 per basic share. The company raised its full-year 2026 revenue growth guidance to approximately 40% or more, up from the prior guidance of approximately 35% or more. CEO Jack Abuhoff stated: Revenue grew 54% year-over-year while Adjusted EBITDA grew approximately 96%. Key highlights include: Adjusted gross margin expanded to 47%. Cash and short-term investments reached $117.4 million. Wells Fargo credit facility expanded to $50 million (undrawn). New Big Tech customer engagements expected to generate approximately $51 million in 2026 revenue. Launch of Evaluation and Observability Platform in beta with initial $1 million engagement. Transition to single-segment reporting reflecting focus on agentic AI technologies. The stock surged 86% on the news, adding approximately $1.38 billion to the company's valuation. The company emphasized that diversification we planned for is now happening in practice as other Big Tech customers' revenue grew 453% year-over-year.
